posts.title: AI News Roundup: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.6 Sol Under Government-Gated Rollout, Trump Signs AI Oversight Executive Order, Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Cloning Claude, Samsung Bets $648 Billion on AI, and the Vatican Enters the AI Ethics Arena — June 28, 2026
posts.excerpt: OpenAI releases GPT-5.6 Sol — but only to government-approved 'trusted partners,' marking the first US-gated AI model rollout. Trump signs an executive order seeking early government access to powerful AI models. Anthropic accuses Alibaba of running 29 million fake queries to clone Claude. Samsung prepares a staggering $648 billion investment plan as AI reshapes South Korea. Qualcomm forecasts $15 billion in data center chip sales by 2029. And Pope Leo XIV launches a Vatican commission on AI ethics.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: US Loosens Anthropic's Mythos Restrictions, OpenAI Poaches Apple's Vision Pro Chief and Leans Toward 2027 IPO, Anthropic and OpenAI Wage $27M Proxy War in Congress, Meta to Replace 90% of Content Reviewers with AI, and Malaysia Seizes $13M in Smuggled AI Chips — June 26, 2026
posts.excerpt: The US government loosens restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos model, clearing it for 'trusted' US organizations. OpenAI poaches Apple's Vision Pro chief and signals its IPO may slip to 2027. Anthropic and OpenAI spent $27 million fighting a proxy war in a Manhattan congressional race. Meta announces plans to replace 90% of its content review staff with AI. And Malaysian customs seizes $13 million in smuggled AI chips — a stark reminder that the hardware arms race has a black market.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: OpenAI Unveils Its First Custom Chip 'Jalapeño', Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of Stealing Claude's Secrets, Google Brings Computer Use to Gemini 3.5 Flash, Qualcomm Buys Modular for $4B, and SpaceX Bets $6.3B on Reflection — June 25, 2026
posts.excerpt: OpenAI unveils Jalapeño, its first custom AI inference chip built with Broadcom. Anthropic accuses Alibaba of a brazen campaign to extract Claude's model capabilities. Google launches computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash. Qualcomm acquires Modular for $4 billion in an AI software push. SpaceX signs a $6.3 billion compute deal with open-source startup Reflection. The AI hardware and security wars are now fully underway.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Anthropic Raises $30B and Poaches a Nobel Laureate, OpenAI's Cyber Model Beats Mythos, Nvidia Becomes World's Most Valuable Company, Oracle Cuts 21K Jobs for AI, and Meta Launches $299 Smart Glasses — June 24, 2026
posts.excerpt: Anthropic closes a monster $30B round and poaches DeepMind's Nobel laureate John Jumper. OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Cyber beats Anthropic's banned Mythos model — and nobody's shutting it down. Nvidia officially becomes the world's most valuable company. Oracle admits AI cost 21,000 jobs. Meta drops $299 smart glasses. The AI arms race isn't slowing down — it's accelerating on every front.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Five Eyes Warns Devastating AI Attacks Are Months Away, Baseten Hits $13B Valuation, OpenAI Fights Open-Source Bugs, Groq Rises From Nvidia's Ashes, and AI Agents Start Running Cafes — June 23, 2026
posts.excerpt: Five Eyes intelligence agencies issue a rare joint warning that AI models capable of devastating cyberattacks on governments and businesses are only months away. Baseten reaches $13B valuation, OpenAI launches an open-source security initiative, Groq confirms $650M raise after Nvidia's talent drain, SpaceX inks a massive compute deal with Reflection AI, and an AI agent is literally running a Swedish cafe.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: Anthropic Demands ID Verification, Trump Escalates Anthropic Crackdown, iOS 27 Brings Practical AI Beyond Siri, Sakana Launches Fugu Multi-Agent Orchestrator, and Open Models Close the Gap — June 22, 2026
posts.excerpt: Anthropic rolls out government ID verification for Claude users, the Trump administration escalates its crackdown on Anthropic while the AI ecosystem asks who benefits, iOS 27 brings practical AI features beyond Siri, Sakana AI launches Fugu — a multi-agent system delivered as one model API — and a compelling case that switching to open models now carries minimal downside.

posts.title: AI News Roundup: SpaceX Acquires Cursor for $60B, Nobel Laureate Jumper Leaves DeepMind for Anthropic, Cloudflare Launches Temporary Accounts for Agents, and GPT-5.5's Hallucination Problem Exposed — June 21, 2026
posts.excerpt: SpaceX's $60B Cursor acquisition reshapes the AI coding wars, Nobel winner John Jumper jumps from DeepMind to Anthropic, Cloudflare lets agents deploy without accounts, Nvidia's ENPIRE framework has AI agents training robots overnight, and a new analysis shows GPT-5.5 hallucinates 3x more than MIT-licensed GLM-5.2.
